title: 7 Herbal Teas For Chills
intro_desc: When you're pregnant, your body goes through lots of changes. One of these changes can be feeling cold or chilled, even if the room is warm. This can happen because your body is working harder to support the growth of your baby, and your blood vessels are expanding to carry more blood to your baby. As a result, you might feel cold or chilly, especially in your hands and feet. This can be uncomfortable and even affect your daily life, making it hard to sleep, work, or even do simple tasks. Some pregnant women might even feel anxious or worried about their health when they feel chilled, which can make the situation even more challenging.  Pregnant women who experience chills might also be more likely to get infections or have other health problems, like low blood pressure or a weakened immune system. This is because their body is already under stress from the pregnancy, and feeling chilled can make things worse. On top of this, some women might be taking medications that can cause side effects like dizziness or fatigue, which can make them feel even more chilled.  Feeling chilled can also affect a pregnant woman's mood and energy levels. Some women might feel tired or irritable, which can make it hard to take care of themselves and their baby. They might also have trouble sleeping or concentrating, which can make everyday tasks feel even more overwhelming.  When a pregnant woman feels chilled, she might try to warm up with a blanket or a hot bath. But sometimes, these remedies don't work, or they might not be comfortable or safe to use while pregnant. This is where herbal teas come in - they can be a natural and soothing way to help a pregnant woman feel better when she's chilled. Herbal teas are made from plants that have been used for centuries to help with all sorts of health issues, including fever and chills. They can help to relax the body and mind, making it easier to fall asleep or feel more comfortable when you're chilled.
faq_1: Drinking herbal tea may help prevent chills from forming. Some herbal teas, like ginger and peppermint, contain natural warming properties that can help increase blood flow and temperature. This can make you feel warmer and more comfortable, especially when you're feeling chilly. The warming sensation can also help to ease any discomfort or shivers.
faq_prevent: Drinking herbal tea may help reduce chills. Some teas, like peppermint and ginger, contain warming compounds that can increase your body temperature. This can make you feel warmer and more comfortable when you're feeling cold. However, the effect of herbal tea on chills can vary from person to person, and it may not work for everyone.
faq_safe: Herbal teas for chills can be a soothing option, but having them every day may not be safe for everyone. Some herbs can interact with medications or have side effects when consumed regularly. For example, ginger and peppermint can cause stomach upset in large amounts. It's best to limit consumption and choose teas from trusted sources.
faq_timeline: Herbal teas can help reduce chills, but it may take a few hours or even a full day to notice results. Some teas, like peppermint and ginger, can start working within 30 minutes to an hour, while others, like chamomile, may take longer. It's best to try different teas to see what works best for you.
faq_time: Drinking herbal tea for chills is best when your body temperature is dropping the fastest. This usually happens in the late afternoon or early evening, around 4-6 pm. At this time, your body is naturally cooling down from the day's activities, and the tea can help you feel warmer and more relaxed.
faq_interaction: Herbal teas can interact with prescription medications for chills. Some herbal teas, like those with ginseng or St. John's Wort, can speed up or slow down the way your body processes medications. This can make the medication less effective or cause more side effects. It's essential to know what's in the herbal tea and talk to your pharmacist.
faq_pregnant: Herbal teas can be a soothing way to ease chills, but be cautious when pregnant. Some herbs like peppermint and ginger are usually safe, but others like sage and pennyroyal may not be. Always check the ingredients and look for warnings about pregnancy. It's better to err on the side of caution and choose a herbal tea specifically designed for pregnant women.
faq_breastfeeding: When you're breastfeeding and feeling chilled, herbal teas can be a soothing option. However, some herbs can pass into breast milk, so choose teas made from gentle ingredients like peppermint or ginger. These teas are less likely to affect your baby, but be cautious with strong herbs like sage or chamomile, which might cause issues.

remedies.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale teas contains compounds like gingerols and shogaols, which are known for their anti-inflammatory and warming properties. These compounds help to increase blood circulation and reduce fever, making ginger tea a popular remedy for chills. When you have chills, your body is trying to fight off an infection. Zingiber officinale teas can help by stimulating your immune system and reducing the severity of symptoms. To take advantage of ginger's warming properties, you can make a tea by steeping fresh ginger in hot water for 5-7 minutes. You can also add honey or lemon to make it more palatable. After drinking the tea, you may start to feel warm and comfortable within 30 minutes to an hour. Your fever may decrease, and you may be able to move more easily. If your chills are accompanied by a fever over 102°F (39°C), or if you're experiencing difficulty breathing, confusion, or severe headaches, you should seek medical attention.
remedies.image_alt: Zingiber officinale herbal teas for chills

remedies.remedy_desc: Echinacea purpurea teas contains properties and constituents that help alleviate chills. The plant contains alkylamides and caffeic acid derivatives, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects that aid in reducing fever and relieving body aches. The tea also contains phenolic compounds like chicoric acid and echinacoside, which have immunomodulatory effects, helping to boost the body's immune response and fight off infections that cause chills.  If you're experiencing chills, you can try drinking Echinacea purpurea tea several times a day, ideally in warm or hot form to help increase blood circulation and relieve body aches. To make the tea, steep one teaspoon of dried Echinacea purpurea root in a cup of boiling water for 5-7 minutes. You can also add honey or lemon to taste.  Taking Echinacea purpurea tea may lead to a reduction in body temperature, relief from fever and body aches, and a boost in immune function. However, it's essential to note that Echinacea purpurea tea is not a substitute for medical treatment. If you're experiencing severe chills or a fever that lasts for more than a few days, you should consult a healthcare professional for proper diagnosis and treatment.
remedies.image_alt: Echinacea purpurea herbal teas for chills

remedies.remedy_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra teas contains anti-inflammatory and antiviral properties that can help alleviate chills. The main constituents, glycyrrhizin and flavonoids, have been shown to reduce inflammation and inhibit the growth of viruses, making them effective in treating fever and chills. Glycyrrhizin, in particular, has been found to have immunomodulatory effects, which can help regulate the immune system and reduce the severity of chills. If you're experiencing chills, try drinking Glycyrrhiza glabra tea when you first feel the symptoms. You can add honey or lemon to taste, but avoid mixing it with other medications without consulting your doctor. To get the most out of the tea, drink it 2-3 times a day, and consider combining it with rest and hydration to help your body recover.  As you drink the tea, you can expect to feel a reduction in your body temperature and a decrease in the severity of your chills. You may also experience a sense of calm and relaxation, thanks to the soothing effects of the tea. Additionally, the anti-inflammatory properties may help reduce muscle and joint pain associated with chills.
remedies.image_alt: Glycyrrhiza glabra herbal teas for chills

remedies.remedy_desc: Eucalyptus globulus teas contains compounds that can help alleviate chills. The main properties are its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory effects, thanks to the presence of eucalyptol, a natural decongestant. Eucalyptol works by thinning mucus, making it easier to expel, and reducing congestion in the body.  To use Eucalyptus globulus tea for chills, you can brew a cup using 1-2 teaspoons of dried leaves in a cup of boiling water for 5-7 minutes. Strain and drink 2-3 times a day. You can also add honey to taste, as it may help soothe a sore throat.  When you take action by drinking Eucalyptus globulus tea regularly, you may start to feel relief from chills within a few hours. Your body should begin to expel excess mucus and congestion, reducing the discomfort caused by chills. You may also experience a decrease in fever and an improvement in your overall well-being.
remedies.image_alt: Eucalyptus globulus herbal teas for chills

remedies.remedy_desc: Sambucus nigra teas contains antiviral and antimicrobial properties that help combat the root cause of chills, which is often a viral or bacterial infection. The main constituents, including sambucol, flavonoids, and anthocyanins, have been shown to inhibit the replication of viruses and bacteria, thereby reducing the severity of symptoms.  To effectively use Sambucus nigra teas for chills, start by drinking 2-3 cups throughout the day, ideally at the onset of symptoms. You can prepare the tea by steeping 1-2 teaspoons of dried elderflower in a cup of boiling water for 5-7 minutes. Strain and sweeten with honey if desired. When taking Sambucus nigra teas, you can expect to feel a reduction in body temperature, a decrease in sweating, and an alleviation of general discomfort. The tea may also help to boost your immune system, making it easier for your body to fight off the underlying infection. Some people may also experience a decrease in the frequency and severity of coughs and sore throats.
remedies.image_alt: Sambucus nigra herbal teas for chills

remedies.remedy_desc: Thymus vulgaris teas contains thymol, a compound that has natural decongestant and antibacterial properties. When you have chills, your body is trying to fight off an infection, and thymol helps by reducing the swelling in your nasal passages and sinuses, making it easier to breathe. Thymol also has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to reduce the fever and alleviate body aches.  To use Thymus vulgaris teas for chills, start by steeping 1 teaspoon of dried thyme leaves in a cup of boiling water for 5-7 minutes. Let it cool down and then drink it slowly. You can also add honey or lemon to taste. Drink the tea 2-3 times a day to help relieve your symptoms. It's also essential to rest and stay hydrated while you're sick, so make sure to drink plenty of fluids and get plenty of sleep. When you drink Thymus vulgaris teas for chills, you can expect your body to start feeling better within a few hours. Your fever should start to subside, and your body aches should decrease. Your nasal congestion should also start to clear up, making it easier to breathe and sleep. However, keep in mind that if your symptoms worsen or last longer than a few days, you should consult with a healthcare professional for proper diagnosis and treatment.
remedies.image_alt: Thymus vulgaris herbal teas for chills

remedies.remedy_desc: Ginkgo biloba teas contains a unique blend of flavonoids, terpenoids, and bilobalide that helps combat chills. The flavonoids, including quercetin and kaempferol, have anti-inflammatory properties that reduce swelling and ease discomfort. The terpenoids, such as ginkgolides A and B, help improve blood flow to the extremities, which can help warm up the body. When you drink Ginkgo biloba tea, these compounds work together to relax blood vessels and promote circulation, making you feel warmer and more comfortable. To take action, try drinking Ginkgo biloba tea when you feel chills coming on. You can steep a tea bag in hot water for 5-7 minutes, then strain and enjoy. You can also add honey or lemon to taste. Start with a small amount, such as 1 cup, and see how your body responds. If you're feeling severely chilled or experiencing other symptoms, consult with a healthcare professional for proper diagnosis and treatment. As you drink the tea, you may start to feel your body warming up within 30 minutes to an hour. You may experience relief from chills and discomfort, and your circulation may improve. The tea may also help alleviate any associated symptoms, such as fever or fatigue. However, keep in mind that Ginkgo biloba tea is not a substitute for medical treatment, and you should consult with a healthcare professional if your symptoms persist or worsen.
remedies.image_alt: Ginkgo biloba herbal teas for chills
